The Porter and the Sailor: Why does Hindbad stop in front of Sinbad's house, and what does he say while sitting on the steps?
Sinbad says the fine house is not what the sea "gave" him — it is what the sea "cost" him. What do you think he means by that?
The First Voyage: The Island That Moved: How does Sinbad end up stranded in the middle of the ocean, and what does he use to stay afloat?
How does Sinbad get his goods back, even though he was thought to be dead?
The Second Voyage: The Valley of Diamond: How does Sinbad escape from the valley of diamonds?
The valley is full of diamonds, but Sinbad almost can't get them out. What two dangers make it so hard to leave?
The Third Voyage: The Giant's Island: What does the giant do each morning, and why can't the sailors simply kill him right away?
The sailors make a plan to escape. Describe the two steps of their plan.
The Fourth Voyage: The Underground Kingd: What is strange about the food given to Sinbad and his companions, and why does Sinbad refuse to eat it?
How does Sinbad escape from the burial chamber underground?
The Fifth Voyage: The Old Man of the Sea: Why does Sinbad let the old man climb on his back, and why is this a mistake?
How does Sinbad finally get the old man off his back?
The Sixth Voyage: The River Under the Mo: Why can't the shipwrecked survivors leave the beach they are trapped on?
What is brave about Sinbad's plan to follow the underground river? What could go wrong?
A Night Off: Hindbad Asks Questions: Hindbad says the whale island was mostly luck, but the diamond valley was mostly skill. Do you agree? Give a reason for your answer.
Sinbad says he went back to sea partly because of the names of the men he lost. What does that tell you about how he felt?
The Seventh Voyage: The King's Letter: Why does Sinbad have to make a seventh voyage even though he wants to stay home?
What does the elephant show Sinbad, and what deal does Sinbad believe the elephant is offering?
Back to the Beginning: Sinbad says he was terrified before his first voyage but would not have admitted it. Why do you think he kept his fear secret?
What lesson does Sinbad say he learned from the kindness of King Mihrjan's people?
The Ship That Did Not Wait: What is the "worst part" of watching the ship leave without him, according to Sinbad?
Sinbad says terrible situations force you to pay attention. Give one example from the story where paying close attention helped him.
What the Giant Taught Me: Sinbad says the giant was not evil — just doing what giants do. What is the point he is trying to make about being a stranger in an unknown place?
What questions does Sinbad say you should ask when something seems too easy or too convenient?
The City Under the Mountain Sky: When the king of Serendib asks Sinbad why he survived, what does Sinbad say? What are the two things he mentions?
Why does the king say he is worried about his own sons?
The Letter the Caliph Sent: Why does Sinbad feel he cannot refuse the Caliph's request, even though he is retired?
How do the pirates capture Sinbad's ship, and what do they do with the prisoners?
The Elephant and the Secret Valley: How does the elephant lead Sinbad to the valley without using words?
What do you think the elephant is offering Sinbad, and what does it want in return?
The Long Way Home: Sinbad describes coming home six different times. Pick one homecoming and explain why it felt different from the others.
What does Sinbad mean when he says coming home the seventh time felt like "a completion"?
What Wealth Actually Costs: Sinbad connects each object in his house to a dangerous event from his voyages. Pick one object he mentions and explain what it cost him.
Hindbad pushes back and says Sinbad had advantages he didn't have. What is Sinbad's answer, and do you think it is fair?
The King of Serendib Remembers: What is the king of Serendib worried his sons have missed by growing up safe and comfortable?
Sinbad says some caves "have to be chosen" and some are just "the only door left open." What does he mean?
The Shape of the Sea: Sinbad says the worst sailors "take the sea personally." What does he mean by this?
According to Sinbad, how is the sea like "most things in life that seem frightening from the outside"?
What Was Lost: Sinbad remembers the names of all eight men who died on the third voyage. Why do you think he kept their names?
Sinbad says he funded the families of the men who died. Why does he say "it was not enough"?
The Merchants He Met: What does the merchant Farouk teach Sinbad about trade? What does he say the sea really is?
Why does Sinbad feel guilty about Daoud, the man on the beach of shipwrecks?
The Last Thing I Learned: What does Sinbad discover about himself during the six-week journey home from the coast?
Hindbad says it is easier to appreciate ordinary life when you have chosen it. Do you agree? Why or why not?
Morning at the Sailor's House: Sinbad says the house would mean nothing if it were all he had to show for his life. What does he say actually matters?
What does Sinbad mean when he says "the house could burn and I would still have it"?
The Purse of Gold: Why does Sinbad give Hindbad a larger purse than the other guests?
At the very end, Hindbad picks up his heavy load and it feels "different" — not lighter, but different. What do you think has changed for him?
